title: Declarations should use Java collection interfaces such as 'List' rather than specific implementation classes like 'LinkedList'
description: The Java Collections API provides a cohesive hierarchy of interfaces that mask the details of collection implementation. It's generally recommended to use these interfaces instead of implementation classes when passing collections as method parameters, return values, or when exposing fields. This rule helps in maintaining this standard.
recommendation: Replace specific implementation classes like 'LinkedList', 'ArrayList', 'HashMap' etc. with their corresponding interface like 'List', 'Set', 'Map' etc. in declarations, parameters and return types.
impact: Using interfaces instead of specific classes increases code flexibility, making it easier to switch to a different implementation in future without changing much of the code. This improves maintainability and code quality.

checks.message: Rule: Declarations should use Java collection interfaces such as 'List' rather than specific implementation classes like 'LinkedList'
